Sine Wave Filter Fault Detection

Early Detection Prevents Catastrophic Pump Failure

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Problem

Electric submersible pumps (ESPs) operate in harsh subsurface environments. A critical component in these systems is the sine wave filter, which helps protect the motor windings from voltage spikes and harmonic distortion.

When a sine wave filter breaks down, it can lead to motor winding insulation failure and complete pump loss. Without proper monitoring, this failure often goes undetected until catastrophic damage occurs.

How Samotics Detected It

Our Electrical Signature Analysis continuously monitors the electrical characteristics of the motor circuit. When the sine wave filter began to degrade, our algorithms detected subtle but significant changes in:

  • Harmonic content in the current waveform
  • Phase relationships between voltage and current
  • Energy efficiency patterns

These early indicators allowed the operator to schedule replacement maintenance before total failure.

The Impact

By catching this fault early, the operator avoided:

  • Complete loss of the pump system
  • Extended downtime for replacement
  • Potential damage to surrounding equipment
  • Loss of production

This single detection paid for the monitoring system several times over.
